title: Compare Fractions Using Number Line
description: Determine which of two fractions is greater by comparing their positions on a number line.
condition: Two fractions are plotted or imagined on the same number line.
response: State that the fraction farther to the right is greater.
label: Principle / Model
example: On a number line, $\frac{3}{6}$ is to the right of $\frac{1}{4}$, so $\frac{3}{6} > \frac{1}{4}$.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Compare Fractions with Common Denominators
description: When two fractions have the same denominator, compare their numerators to determine which fraction is greater.
condition: Two fractions with equal denominators.
response: The fraction with the greater numerator is greater; the one with the smaller numerator is less.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compare $\frac{4}{6}$ and $\frac{5}{6}$. Since $4 < 5$, $\frac{4}{6} < \frac{5}{6}$.
notes: Used after renaming fractions to like denominators.

title: Compare Fractions with Common Numerator
description: When two fractions have the same numerator, the fraction with the smaller denominator is greater because the whole is partitioned into fewer, larger parts.
condition: Two fractions a/b and a/c with the same numerator and different denominators.
response: Determine that the fraction with the smaller denominator is greater.
label: Principle
example: Compare $\frac{7}{6}$ and $\frac{7}{3}$. Since $6 > 3$, sixths are smaller parts than thirds, so $\frac{7}{6} < \frac{7}{3}$.

title: Compare Fractions with Same-Sized Wholes
description: Determine which fraction is greater when the wholes are the same size by comparing numerators (for like denominators) or using a number line.
condition: Two fractions referring to the same-sized whole are given for comparison.
response: Identify which fraction is greater, less, or equal and justify using size or position on a number line.
label: Principle / Model
example: Since $\frac{2}{3} > \frac{1}{3}$, Gabe eats more than Robin.

title: Compare Unit Fractions
description: Determine which of two unit fractions is greater by reasoning about the size of equal parts of the same whole.
condition: Two unit fractions (form 1/n) referring to the same whole (e.g., 1/2 and 1/3).
response: State which fraction is greater or less based on denominator size (smaller denominator → larger fraction).
label: Principle
example: Compare $\frac{1}{2}$ and $\frac{1}{3}$. Since halves are larger pieces than thirds, $\frac{1}{2} > \frac{1}{3}$.
notes: Requires understanding that more equal parts means smaller pieces.

title: Count Equal Partitions on Number Line
description: Determine the denominator of a fraction by counting equal parts between 0 and 1.
condition: A number line from 0 to 1 partitioned into equal intervals.
response: Count the total number of equal parts to determine the denominator.
label: Concept
example: If the interval from 0 to 1 is divided into 8 equal parts, the denominator is 8.

title: Define a Unit Fraction
description: Recognize that a unit fraction represents one equal part of a whole partitioned into equal parts.
condition: A fraction of the form $\frac{1}{n}$ is given.
response: Interpret it as 1 out of $n$ equal parts of a whole.
label: Concept
example: $\frac{1}{4}$ means 1 of 4 equal parts.

title: Denominator as Number of Equal Parts
description: Interpret the denominator as the total number of equal parts in one whole.
condition: A whole is partitioned into equal parts.
response: State that the denominator equals the total number of equal parts.
label: Principle / Model
example: If a square is divided into 4 equal parts, each part is $\frac{1}{4}$.

title: Fraction as Multiple of Unit Fraction
description: Interpret a fraction a/b as a copies of 1/b.
condition: A fraction with numerator greater than 1 is given.
response: Interpret it as that many copies of the unit fraction.
label: Principle / Model
example: $\frac{3}{4}$ means 3 copies of $\frac{1}{4}$.

title: Identify Shapes Partitioned into Equal Parts
description: Recognize whether a shape has been divided into parts of equal size.
condition: A shape is partitioned into multiple regions.
response: Determine whether all regions are equal in size.
label: Category
example: A circle divided into 6 congruent slices is partitioned into equal parts.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Interpret Fraction as Equal Parts of a Whole
description: Understand that a fraction a/b represents a equal parts out of b equal parts that make one whole.
condition: A fraction symbol of the form a/b is given.
response: Interpret it as a parts out of b equal parts of one whole.
label: Rule / Plan
example: $\frac{5}{6}$ means 5 equal parts out of 6 equal parts that make one whole.

title: Interpret Numerator as Count of Selected Parts
description: The numerator tells how many equal parts are selected or shaded.
condition: A fraction a/b describes parts of a whole partitioned into b equal parts.
response: Select or count a parts.
label: Principle
example: For $\frac{3}{4}$, shade 3 of the 4 equal parts.

title: Label Specified Fraction on Partitioned Number Line
description: Place or label a given fraction at the correct position on a number line divided into equal parts.
condition: Instruction to label a specific fraction (e.g., 1/3) on a number line from 0 to 1.
response: Partition the interval into the denominator’s number of equal parts and mark the numerator-th part from 0.
label: Principle
example: To label $\frac{1}{3}$, divide 0–1 into 3 equal parts and mark the first tick after 0.

title: Locate Improper Fraction on Number Line
description: Place a fraction greater than 1 on a number line by counting whole intervals and remaining fractional parts.
condition: An improper fraction a/b where a > b must be located on a number line partitioned into b equal parts per whole.
response: Count how many whole numbers fit into a/b and place the point accordingly.
label: Principle
example: $\frac{5}{3}$ equals $1\frac{2}{3}$, so it is located two thirds past 1 on the number line.

title: Match Visual Model to Unit Fraction
description: Match a partitioned and shaded model to the corresponding unit fraction.
condition: A shape or strip shows 1 shaded part out of $n$ equal parts.
response: Select or state the fraction $\frac{1}{n}$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 1 of 8 equal parts is shaded, the fraction is $\frac{1}{8}$.
notes: Integrates counting equal parts and unit fraction meaning.

title: Partition Whole into Equal Parts
description: Divide a whole shape or number line interval into a specified number of equal parts.
condition: Instruction to divide a whole into n equal parts.
response: Create n equal partitions of the whole.
label: Production / Skill
example: Partition the interval from 0 to 1 into 3 equal parts to show thirds.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Recognize Fraction Equal to a Whole Number
description: A fraction with numerator equal to the denominator equals 1 whole; a fraction where the numerator is a multiple of the denominator equals a whole number.
condition: A fraction where the numerator equals or is a multiple of the denominator (e.g., a/a or ka/a).
response: State the equivalent whole number.
label: Principle / Model
example: $\frac{4}{4} = 1$ and $\frac{16}{8} = 2$ because the numerator is a multiple of the denominator.

title: Select Appropriate Comparison Symbol
description: Choose >, <, or = to correctly compare two numbers.
condition: Two numbers with a known relative size.
response: Write > if the first is greater, < if less, or = if equal.
label: Concept
example: Since $\frac{3}{6} < \frac{3}{4}$, write $\frac{3}{6} < \frac{3}{4}$.

title: Select Model Showing Valid Fraction Comparison
description: Choose a visual model that correctly represents two fractions of the same whole to justify a comparison statement.
condition: Multiple visual models are provided to justify a comparison between two fractions.
response: Select the model where both fractions refer to equal-sized wholes and are partitioned appropriately.
label: Concept
example: To show $\frac{1}{2} > \frac{1}{3}$, select two equal-length bars partitioned into 2 and 3 equal parts respectively.
notes: Response is constant per item (e.g., option C in Q4).

title: Shade Numerator Number of Parts
description: Shade the number of equal parts indicated by the numerator once the whole is partitioned.
condition: A whole partitioned into b equal parts and a fraction a/b is given.
response: Shade exactly a of the equal parts.
label: Production / Skill
example: For $\frac{5}{6}$, after dividing into 6 parts, shade 5 parts.

title: Sort Shapes by Number of Equal Parts
description: Classify shapes based on how many equal parts they are partitioned into.
condition: Multiple shapes are shown with different partitions.
response: Group each shape according to whether it is partitioned into thirds, fourths, sixths, or not equal parts.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If a circle is divided into 6 equal sectors, place it under 'Partitioned into Sixths.'
notes: Requires counting and checking equality of parts.

title: Write Whole Number as Fraction Over 1
description: Any whole number n can be written as n/1.
condition: A whole number is to be expressed as a fraction.
response: Write the number as n/1.
label: Principle
